title: Day/night cycle
goal: Advance Lighting.ClockTime every frame for a fast, visible day/night loop.

steps.body: `Lighting.ClockTime` is a number between 0 and 24 that sets the in-game time of day. The engine maps it to sun position, sky gradient, and ambient color. You don't have to touch a single material or light source — advancing the clock is enough.
Kick things off at dusk so the first frame looks dramatic. You can see the same field under Lighting in the Explorer — everything below the hood is just properties.
Bump Brightness so the sun reads more strongly against the sky gradient.
A server Script under ServerScriptService drives the clock. Heartbeat fires every tick with the real elapsed time — we multiply to compress a full in-game day into a short window.
steps.source:
-- Advance Lighting.ClockTime by Heartbeat. HOURS_PER_REAL_SECOND controls
-- how fast the in-game day runs; 1 real second = 1 in-game hour at 1.0.
local Lighting = game:GetService("Lighting")
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")

local HOURS_PER_REAL_SECOND = 1.0

RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
	Lighting.ClockTime = (Lighting.ClockTime + dt * HOURS_PER_REAL_SECOND) % 24
end)

steps.body: Bump the speed to 3 hours per real second. The sky transition now reads clearly while you watch.
steps.source:
-- Advance Lighting.ClockTime by Heartbeat. HOURS_PER_REAL_SECOND controls
-- how fast the in-game day runs; 1 real second = 1 in-game hour at 1.0.
local Lighting = game:GetService("Lighting")
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")

local HOURS_PER_REAL_SECOND = 3.0

RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
	Lighting.ClockTime = (Lighting.ClockTime + dt * HOURS_PER_REAL_SECOND) % 24
end)

steps.body: Press ▶ Play (F5). Watch the sky cycle from dusk → night → dawn in a few seconds. Shift+F5 to stop and tweak `HOURS_PER_REAL_SECOND` if the pace isn't right for what you're building.
